title: PortableCarrier
description: A candidate portable self-continuity carrier — any hypothesized mechanism that preserves the inhabited ownership-stream (C, P, O, B, K, N) across substrate transition. Most claims here are unproven. This schema documents what a carrier must do and evaluates whether proposed carriers satisfy the requirements.

mechanism_description.description: How this carrier is proposed to work. Must be mechanistic. 'The soul carries it' is not a mechanism — 'the soul implements C through non-physical substrate continuity' is.

components_claimed.description: Which S-components this carrier is claimed to preserve. true = claimed to preserve; false = known to fail; null = not addressed by proponents.

what_it_preserves.description: What this carrier genuinely preserves — even if insufficient for full self-continuity.

what_it_fails_to_preserve.description: Which S-components this carrier demonstrably fails to carry.

fission_problem.description: How this carrier handles the fission/copy problem. Can it guarantee N=1 (non-branching)?

empirical_status.description: Current empirical standing of this carrier claim.

verdict.description: Assessment of whether this carrier satisfies the requirements for self-continuity preservation.

revision_pressure.description: What this carrier claim puts pressure on in the theory.
